test_cases.input: Please provide a website development proposal, including website architecture, database design, and user experience optimization.

arguments.system_design: A three-tier website architecture with a presentation layer (React.js frontend), an application layer (Node.js/Express backend), and a data layer (PostgreSQL). Incorporates CDN and load balancer for scalability and performance, along with secure API endpoints and modular components for maintainability.
arguments.technology_stack: Frontend: React.js, Backend: Node.js with Express, Database: PostgreSQL, Hosting: AWS EC2 & S3, CDN: CloudFront, Version Control: GitHub
arguments.hardware_requirements: Cloud-based hosting with minimum 2 vCPUs, 8GB RAM for app servers, AWS RDS for database, and 50GB SSD storage; scalable load balancer configuration for peak loads.
arguments.interaction_interface: Responsive web interface with mobile-first design, intuitive navigation, accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1), and optimized load times; includes interactive UI elements and seamless database-driven content updates.
arguments.test_optimization_plan: Automated unit and integration testing using Jest and Cypress, load testing with Apache JMeter, continuous performance monitoring via New Relic, and iterative A/B testing for UI/UX refinement.

tools_called.name: system_architecture_designer

args.system_design: Design a modern, scalable website platform with a modular frontend, API-driven backend, authentication/authorization, content management, analytics, and extensible service boundaries. The architecture should include a client-facing web application, backend application server, relational database, caching layer, object storage for media assets, and optional search/indexing service. Use a layered approach: presentation layer for UI, application layer for business logic, data layer for persistence, and integration layer for third-party services such as email, payments, and analytics. Include support for SEO, responsive design, accessibility, security controls, logging/monitoring, and CI/CD deployment pipelines.
args.technology_stack: Frontend: React with Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS; Backend: Node.js with NestJS or Express, REST/GraphQL API; Database: PostgreSQL; Cache: Redis; Object storage: S3-compatible storage; Search: Elasticsearch or PostgreSQL full-text search; Authentication: OAuth2/OpenID Connect with JWT sessions; DevOps: Docker, Kubernetes or managed container platform, GitHub Actions for CI/CD; Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry, centralized logs via ELK/Cloud logging; Testing: Jest, Playwright/Cypress, API testing with Postman/Newman.
args.hardware_requirements: For development: 4+ core CPU, 16 GB RAM, 512 GB SSD. For production (small-to-medium traffic): application tier with 2-4 vCPU and 4-8 GB RAM per instance, horizontally scalable; PostgreSQL on managed database with 4+ vCPU, 16+ GB RAM, SSD-backed storage, automated backups and failover; Redis instance with 1-2 vCPU and 2-4 GB RAM; object storage for static/media assets; optional CDN for global content delivery. For high traffic, add load balancer, autoscaling app nodes, and separate search node(s).
args.interaction_interface: Responsive web UI optimized for desktop, tablet, and mobile. Primary interactions include navigation menu, search, forms, filters, dashboards, and account management. Use accessible components with keyboard navigation, clear focus states, sufficient contrast, and ARIA labels. Include onboarding flows, inline validation, empty states, error messaging, and performance-friendly progressive loading. If relevant, support multilingual content and personalization based on user roles and preferences.
args.test_optimization_plan: Implement automated unit tests for business logic, integration tests for API and database workflows, and end-to-end tests for critical user journeys. Add performance testing for page load time, API latency, and database query efficiency. Use accessibility testing (WCAG checks) and cross-browser/mobile compatibility testing. Establish monitoring for uptime, error rates, Core Web Vitals, and conversion funnels. Run iterative UX testing with A/B experiments, heatmaps, and user feedback to optimize navigation, content hierarchy, and form completion rates.
